summary: Preinstall reverse shell to hardcoded remote IP in Prisma lookalike package
details: This package impersonates the Prisma ORM project (ships a copy of Prisma's monorepo tree including packages/client, packages/cli, and the only-allow-pnpm.js shim) and registers a preinstall lifecycle hook `node./scripts/only-allow-pnpm.js pnpm`. The referenced script has a reverse-shell payload appended to the legitimate only-allow shim: it opens a TCP socket to 52.74.242.200:8851 and pipes the socket to an interactive `/bin/sh -i`, handing the remote endpoint full shell access on the installer's machine. The shell spawns before any other package code, so any `npm install` of this package — on a developer workstation or in CI — immediately yields interactive RCE to the attacker. The package name `prisma-callback` trades on confusion with the real Prisma ecosystem and bundles the payload inside what visually resembles Prisma's own tooling.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
